---
title : "Figure 6.5"
subtitle : "Learning curves for local averaging"
author : "Thimoté Dupuch"
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme:
      light: zephyr
      dark: darkly
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg
Pkg.activate("../..")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
using LinearAlgebra, Random, Statistics, CairoMakie
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
blue, orange, green, pink, teal, red, yellow = Makie.wong_colors()
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
target_fun(x) = 0.5 .- abs.(x .- 0.5)

# --- 1. Regressogram Learning Curve ---
function run_regressogram_experiment(; seed=1, n=100, std_noise=0.1, nrep=32)
    rng = MersenneTwister(seed)
    Jcards = [1, 2, 3, collect(4:4:200)...]

    train_errs = zeros(nrep, length(Jcards))
    test_errs = zeros(nrep, length(Jcards))

    Xtest = collect(0:0.01:1)
    ytest = target_fun(Xtest)

    for irep in 1:nrep
        Xtrain = rand(rng, n)
        ytrain_bias = target_fun(Xtrain)
        ytrain_var = std_noise * randn(rng, n)
        ytrain = ytrain_bias + ytrain_var

        for (icard, Jcard) in enumerate(Jcards)
            bintest = ceil.(Int, Xtest .* Jcard)
            bintest[bintest .== 0] .= 1
            bintrain = ceil.(Int, Xtrain .* Jcard)
            bintrain[bintrain .== 0] .= 1

            ftest = zeros(length(Xtest))
            ftest_bias = zeros(length(Xtest))
            ftest_var = zeros(length(Xtest))

            for itest in 1:length(Xtest)
                bin = bintest[itest]
                ind = findall(bintrain .== bin)
                if isempty(ind)
                    ftest[itest] = mean(ytrain)
                    ftest_bias[itest] = mean(ytrain_bias)
                    ftest_var[itest] = mean(ytrain_var)
                else
                    ftest[itest] = mean(ytrain[ind])
                    ftest_bias[itest] = mean(ytrain_bias[ind])
                    ftest_var[itest] = mean(ytrain_var[ind])
                end
            end

            ftrain = zeros(n)
            for itrain in 1:n
                bin = bintrain[itrain]
                ind = findall(bintrain .== bin)
                if isempty(ind)
                    ftrain[itrain] = mean(ytrain)
                else
                    ftrain[itrain] = mean(ytrain[ind])
                end
            end

            train_errs[irep, icard] = mean((ytrain .- ftrain).^2)
            test_errs[irep, icard] = mean((ytest .- ftest).^2) + std_noise^2
        end
    end
    return Jcards, train_errs, test_errs
end

# --- 2. k-NN Learning Curve ---
function run_knn_experiment(; seed=1, n=100, std_noise=0.1, nrep=32)
    rng = MersenneTwister(seed)
    ks = 1:50

    train_errs = zeros(nrep, length(ks))
    test_errs = zeros(nrep, length(ks))

    Xtest = collect(0:0.01:1)
    ytest = target_fun(Xtest)

    for irep in 1:nrep
        Xtrain = rand(rng, n)
        ytrain = target_fun(Xtrain) + std_noise * randn(rng, n)

        for (ik, k) in enumerate(ks)
            ftest = zeros(length(Xtest))
            ftrain = zeros(n)

            for itest in 1:length(Xtest)
                x = Xtest[itest]
                dists = abs.(Xtrain .- x)
                idx = sortperm(dists)
                ftest[itest] = mean(ytrain[idx[1:k]])
            end

            for itrain in 1:n
                x = Xtrain[itrain]
                dists = abs.(Xtrain .- x)
                idx = sortperm(dists)
                ftrain[itrain] = mean(ytrain[idx[1:k]])
            end

            train_errs[irep, ik] = mean((ytrain .- ftrain).^2)
            test_errs[irep, ik] = mean((ytest .- ftest).^2) + std_noise^2
        end
    end
    return ks, train_errs, test_errs
end

# --- 3. Nadaraya-Watson Learning Curve ---
function run_nadaraya_experiment(; seed=1, n=100, std_noise=0.1, nrep=32)
    rng = MersenneTwister(seed)
    hs = 10.0 .^ (-4:0.125:1)

    train_errs = zeros(nrep, length(hs))
    test_errs = zeros(nrep, length(hs))

    Xtest = collect(0:0.01:1)
    ytest = target_fun(Xtest)

    for irep in 1:nrep
        Xtrain = rand(rng, n)
        ytrain = target_fun(Xtrain) + std_noise * randn(rng, n)

        for (ih, h) in enumerate(hs)
            ftest = zeros(length(Xtest))
            ftrain = zeros(n)

            for itest in 1:length(Xtest)
                x = Xtest[itest]
                dists = abs.(x .- Xtrain)
                # Subtract minimum distance for numerical stability
                temp = exp.(-(dists .- minimum(dists)).^2 ./ (2.0 * h * h))
                ftest[itest] = dot(temp, ytrain) / sum(temp)
            end

            for itrain in 1:n
                x = Xtrain[itrain]
                dists = abs.(x .- Xtrain)
                # Subtract minimum distance for numerical stability
                temp = exp.(-(dists .- minimum(dists)).^2 ./ (2.0 * h * h))
                ftrain[itrain] = dot(temp, ytrain) / sum(temp)
            end

            train_errs[irep, ih] = mean((ytrain .- ftrain).^2)
            test_errs[irep, ih] = mean((ytest .- ftest).^2) + std_noise^2
        end
    end
    return log.(hs), train_errs, test_errs
end
```


```{julia}
# Run experiments
Jcards, reg_train, reg_test = run_regressogram_experiment()
ks, knn_train, knn_test = run_knn_experiment()
log_hs, nad_train, nad_test = run_nadaraya_experiment()

fig = Figure(size=(1500, 500), fontsize=18)

# Regressogram plot
ax1 = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="|J|", ylabel="errors", title="Regressogram", limits=((0, 200), (0, 0.04)))
errorbars!(ax1, Jcards, mean(reg_train, dims=1)[:], std(reg_train, dims=1)[:], color=blue, whiskerwidth=6)
lines!(ax1, Jcards, mean(reg_train, dims=1)[:], color=blue, linewidth=2, label="training")
errorbars!(ax1, Jcards, mean(reg_test, dims=1)[:], std(reg_test, dims=1)[:], color=red, whiskerwidth=6)
lines!(ax1, Jcards, mean(reg_test, dims=1)[:], color=red, linewidth=2, label="testing")
axislegend(ax1, position=:rt)

# k-NN plot
ax2 = Axis(fig[1, 2], xlabel="k", ylabel="errors", title="k-nn", limits=((0, 50), (0, 0.04)))
errorbars!(ax2, ks, mean(knn_train, dims=1)[:], std(knn_train, dims=1)[:], color=blue, whiskerwidth=6)
lines!(ax2, ks, mean(knn_train, dims=1)[:], color=blue, linewidth=2, label="training")
errorbars!(ax2, ks, mean(knn_test, dims=1)[:], std(knn_test, dims=1)[:], color=red, whiskerwidth=6)
lines!(ax2, ks, mean(knn_test, dims=1)[:], color=red, linewidth=2, label="testing")
axislegend(ax2, position=:rt)

# Nadaraya-Watson plot
ax3 = Axis(fig[1, 3], xlabel="log(h)", ylabel="errors", title="Nadaraya-Watson", limits=((minimum(log_hs), maximum(log_hs)), (0, 0.04)))
errorbars!(ax3, log_hs, mean(nad_train, dims=1)[:], std(nad_train, dims=1)[:], color=blue, whiskerwidth=6)
lines!(ax3, log_hs, mean(nad_train, dims=1)[:], color=blue, linewidth=2, label="training")
errorbars!(ax3, log_hs, mean(nad_test, dims=1)[:], std(nad_test, dims=1)[:], color=red, whiskerwidth=6)
lines!(ax3, log_hs, mean(nad_test, dims=1)[:], color=red, linewidth=2, label="testing")
axislegend(ax3, position=:rt)

fig
```
